meaning: n.喳喳声,唧唧声v.吱喳而鸣,尖声地说
examples: The chirp came from a thick holly bush, bright with scarlet berries, and Mary thought she knew whose it was.
At this year's CHIRP Conference, Twitter revealed that it's attempting to address some of these very issues.
Panksepp has studied rats and found that when they play, they often chirp—a primitive form of laughter, according to the scientist.
The Forbes Chirp Index, which in December predicted an economic upswing, has changed its tune.
You can tell the temperature (in Fahrenheit degrees) by listening to a cricket chirp.
